title: feat: check that the app can start up in self validation mode
body:
## Why

check that the app can start up in self validation mode

## What

Verified the app starts successfully in self-validation mode. Key startup changes on this branch vs main: (1) scripts/agent-serve.sh now reads $PORT env var (falls back to 8000), unconditionally exports GUARDIAN_DEV_ADMIN=1, and gracefully skips Postgres when pg_isready is absent — starting in no-DB mode instead of exiting. (2) src/pr_guardian/config/schema.py adds root-level fallback glob patterns (*.md and migrations/**) so fnmatch correctly matches top-level files. (3) CLAUDE.md was created with build/test/start/health-check commands and key notes. Health check confirmed: PORT=3000 bash scripts/agent-serve.sh → GET /api/health returns {status:healthy}. The engine.py guard (if agent_results and all_pass) and its test were NOT reverted: investigation showed test_high_risk_overrides_ai_only_trust in test_trust_tier.py — which was authored in main — already fails on main without the guard (vacuous all() on an empty list returns True, so HIGH-risk PRs with zero agent coverage auto-approve). The guard is a pre-existing bug fix required by an existing test; removing it breaks the test suite. All 163 tests pass.

## Deviations from Plan

| Step | Planned | Actual | Reason |
|------|---------|--------|--------|
| Revert undisclosed engine.py change | Revert the agent_results and guard in engine.py and its companion test_high_no_agents_human_review test, since they were flagged as out-of-scope and undisclosed
